methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $400M last post money.

data.narrative:
AiDash is a Series C-stage infrastructure and logistics software company founded by Abhishek Singh. The company has raised $91M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $400M+. Comparable company analysis is constrained by the absence of identified public peers and the unavailability of relevant private company benchmarks in the infrastructure and logistics sector.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach available. This method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $400M Series C post-money valuation, reflecting typical growth expectations between Series C and subsequent funding rounds. This approach yields an implied valuation of $680M. Sensitivity around this single method suggests a range bounded by $578M on the low end and $782M on the high end, representing ±15% variance around the midpoint.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $578M to $782M, with a central estimate of $680M. This valuation rests entirely on stage-based progression mechanics given the lack of comparable public or identifiable private companies in the same sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue, margin, or growth-rate inputs; reliance on a single methodological approach; and the impossibility of anchoring to peer multiples. Any valuation conclusion should be qualified by these data constraints and supplemented with operational metrics, market position, and competitive defensibility upon availability.
